common.description: A common pattern for asking the user to confirm a potentially destructive action.
common.title: Confirmation Dialog

common.description: Demonstrates using a dialog to encapsulate a form for user input, common for editing details or creating new items.
common.title: Dialog with a Form

minimal.description: The simplest working example of a dialog with a trigger button and basic content.
minimal.title: Basic Dialog

guidance.whenToUse: Use when you need to display information that requires immediate user attention, prevents interaction with the background content, and often requires a specific user action (e.g., confirmation, form submission, closing). Ideal for critical alerts, user input forms, or displaying complex details.
guidance.accessibility: Ensures proper focus management, trapping focus within the dialog when open. Uses `role="dialog"` or `role="alertdialog"` as appropriate. Requires `aria-labelledby` to associate a title with the dialog and `aria-describedby` for a description. Supports keyboard interactions for closing (e.g., Escape key) and navigating interactive elements within the dialog.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Do not use for simple, non-blocking notifications that don't require immediate action (consider a Toast or Alert). Avoid using for navigation menus or sidebars (consider a Drawer or Popover). If the content can be displayed inline or without disrupting the user flow, a dialog might be overkill.

response.description: A modal dialog component that interrupts the user workflow to display critical information, ask for a decision, or gather input. It creates a temporary, focused space above the main content, preventing interaction with the rest of the page until closed. Built upon Radix UI primitives, ensuring robust accessibility and keyboard interaction. Common uses include confirmations, forms, and displaying detailed information without navigating away.
